bas_/Showcase
FORMAT LAB · ALL SUPPORTED SYNTAX

THE
KITCHEN
SINK

Code blocks. Terminal sessions. Mermaid diagrams. LaTeX formulas. Dataview entries. Callouts. Tables. Every format this site can render — on one page.

01Code Blocks
02Terminal
03Mermaid Diagrams
04LaTeX / Math
05Dataview Entries
06Callouts
07Typography
01 CODE BLOCKS Shiki · vesper theme · server-side
GO handler.go 
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"
	"time"
)

type Handler struct {
	db     *DB
	logger *slog.Logger
}

func (h *Handler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(r.Context(), 5*time.Second)
	defer cancel()

	rows, err := h.db.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT id, body FROM posts LIMIT 20")
	if err != nil {
		h.logger.ErrorContext(ctx, "db query failed", "err", err)
		http.Error(w, "internal error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	defer rows.Close()

	// stream NDJSON — no buffering, no allocation per row
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/x-ndjson")
	enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
	for rows.Next() {
		var p Post
		if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.Body); err != nil {
			break
		}
		enc.Encode(p) // flush per row
	}
}
RUST alloc.rs 
use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

/// Tracking allocator — wraps the system allocator and counts bytes in flight.
pub struct TrackingAllocator {
    inner: System,
    allocated: AtomicUsize,
}

unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for TrackingAllocator {
    unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {
        let ptr = self.inner.alloc(layout);
        if !ptr.is_null() {
            self.allocated.fetch_add(layout.size(), Ordering::Relaxed);
        }
        ptr
    }

    unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {
        self.inner.dealloc(ptr, layout);
        self.allocated.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::Relaxed);
    }
}

impl TrackingAllocator {
    pub fn live_bytes(&self) -> usize {
        self.allocated.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
    }
}

#[global_allocator]
static ALLOC: TrackingAllocator = TrackingAllocator {
    inner: System,
    allocated: AtomicUsize::new(0),
};
BASH publish.sh 
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# publish — build, fingerprint, and push to Cloudflare Pages
set -euo pipefail

SITE_DIR="public-site"
VAULT_DIR="vault-site"

log() { printf '\033[1;33m[publish]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
die() { printf '\033[1;31m[error]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }

[[ -d "$SITE_DIR" ]] || die "not in monorepo root"

log "cleaning dist..."
rm -rf "$SITE_DIR/dist" "$VAULT_DIR/public"

log "building public site..."
(cd "$SITE_DIR" && npm run build)

log "building vault..."
(cd "$VAULT_DIR" && npx quartz build)

log "deploying to Cloudflare Pages..."
wrangler pages deploy "$SITE_DIR/dist" --project-name bas-dev
wrangler pages deploy "$VAULT_DIR/public" --project-name vault-bas-dev

log "done. propagation takes ~30s"
02 TERMINAL SESSION Shell output · git · system inspection
bash · ~/Projects/Website/public-site
bas@arch ~/Projects/Website [main] $ git log --oneline -6 a4f2c91 feat: add showcase page with full format support 3d88e14 chore: switch fonts to Bebas Neue + IBM Plex Mono c19a702 feat: underground aesthetic overhaul 8b3e041 fix: strip .mdx extension from entry slugs 2ff1d0a feat: phase 4 — all layouts and pages 1a9c338 feat: phase 3 — wikilink remark plugin bas@arch ~/Projects/Website [main] $ npm run build > public-site@0.0.1 build > astro build 19:14:22 [content] Syncing content 19:14:22 [content] Synced content 19:14:22 [build] output: "static" 19:14:22 [build] ✓ Completed in 412ms. generating static routes ▶ src/pages/index.astro └─ /index.html (+3ms) ▶ src/pages/blog/index.astro └─ /blog/index.html (+6ms) ▶ src/pages/projects/index.astro └─ /projects/index.html (+3ms) ▶ src/pages/about.astro └─ /about/index.html (+9ms) ▶ src/pages/showcase.astro └─ /showcase/index.html (+14ms) 19:14:24 [build] 7 page(s) built in 2.44s 19:14:24 [build] Complete! bas@arch ~/Projects/Website [main] $ du -sh dist/ 1.2M dist/ $ cat /proc/meminfo | grep -E "MemAvailable|Cached" MemAvailable: 6432108 kB Cached: 3218944 kB SwapCached: 0 kB
03 MERMAID DIAGRAMS mermaid.js · client-side · SVG output
FLOWCHART Deploy pipeline 
flowchart TD
  A([git push]) --> B{"CI checks"}
  B -->|pass| C[astro build]
  B -->|fail| X([blocked])
  C --> D[quartz build]
  D --> E{"env?"}
  E -->|prod| F[wrangler deploy]
  E -->|preview| G[preview URL]
  F --> H([bas.dev live])
  G --> I([preview.bas.dev])
  style A fill:#d43018,color:#e2dfd9,stroke:none
  style H fill:#3a8a4a,color:#e2dfd9,stroke:none
  style X fill:#383532,color:#9a9691,stroke:none
SEQUENCE HTTP request lifecycle 
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as Client
  participant E as Edge Cache
  participant O as Origin
  participant D as Database
  C->>E: GET /blog/post
  alt cache hit
    E-->>C: 200 (cached)
  else cache miss
    E->>O: forward request
    O->>D: SELECT * FROM posts
    D-->>O: rows
    O-->>E: 200 + Cache-Control
    E-->>C: 200 (fresh)
  end
GIT GRAPH Branch strategy 
gitGraph
  commit id: "init"
  branch feat/vault
  checkout feat/vault
  commit id: "quartz config"
  commit id: "custom layout"
  checkout main
  branch feat/public
  commit id: "astro bootstrap"
  commit id: "phase 4 pages"
  checkout main
  merge feat/vault id: "merge vault"
  merge feat/public id: "merge public"
  commit id: "deploy"
PIE CHART Memory allocation 
pie title Memory allocation sources
  "Stack frames" : 38
  "Heap (managed)" : 29
  "mmap regions" : 18
  "Code segment" : 9
  "BSS / data" : 6
04 LATEX / MATH KaTeX · auto-render · display + inline
Cache efficiency — Amdahl's law applied to memory hierarchy
$$T_{eff} = h \cdot T_{hit} + (1 - h) \cdot T_{miss}$$
where $h$ is the cache hit rate, $T_{hit}$ is L1 latency (~4 cycles), $T_{miss}$ is DRAM latency (~200 cycles)
Throughput under Little's Law — requests in flight
$$\lambda = \frac{N}{W} \quad \Leftrightarrow \quad N = \lambda \cdot W$$
$N$ = mean concurrency, $\lambda$ = arrival rate (req/s), $W$ = mean latency (s)
Buddy allocator — number of blocks at level k
$$\text{blocks}(k) = \left\lfloor \frac{M}{2^k \cdot B} \right\rfloor, \quad k \in [0,\, K_{\max}]$$
$M$ = heap size, $B$ = minimum block size, $K_{max} = \log_2(M/B)$
Entropy of a Huffman-coded symbol stream
$$H(X) = -\sum_{i=1}^{n} p_i \log_2 p_i \quad \text{bits/symbol}$$
Shannon entropy lower-bounds the expected code length for any prefix-free encoding
Inline math — mixed with prose
A binary search tree with $n$ nodes has expected lookup time $O(\log n)$ in the average case, but degenerates to $O(n)$ on sorted input — hence the need for self-balancing variants. The AVL invariant requires $|h_L - h_R| \leq 1$ at every node, guaranteeing $h \leq 1.44 \log_2(n+2)$ for a tree of height $h$.
05 DATAVIEW ENTRIES Obsidian Dataview syntax · static HTML output
DATAVIEW TABLE query
TABLE title, status, date, file.tags FROM "vault/projects" SORT date DESC
File Title Status Date Tags
pi-homelab Pi Homelab — NixOS self-hosted infra shipped 2024-11-14 systems linux
mutt-workflow Mutt + mbsync mail pipeline wip 2025-01-08 linux foss
zig-allocator Arena allocator in Zig wip 2025-02-20 systems learning
ocaml-parser S-expression parser · OCaml archived 2023-08-11 systems
DATAVIEW LIST query
LIST FROM #seedling WHERE type = "note" SORT file.ctime DESC LIMIT 5
  • Context switch cost — indirect costs of preemption in the Linux scheduler NOV '25
  • mmap vs malloc — when the kernel's directly faster than your allocator OCT '25
  • Zig comptime — static dispatch without monomorphisation cost SEP '25
  • POSIX lies — things Linux does that POSIX doesn't promise AUG '25
06 CALLOUTS Four variants — inline with prose
⚠ WARNING
Never call mmap in a tight loop. Each call crosses the kernel boundary and may trigger a TLB shootdown on multi-core systems. Batch your mappings or use a slab.
ℹ NOTE
The Linux kernel uses a combination of buddy allocation and slab allocation. The buddy system handles coarse-grained page allocation; slabs handle small, frequently-allocated kernel objects.
✓ TIP
Use perf stat -e cache-misses,cache-references to measure your actual cache pressure before optimising. Guessing is slower than measuring.
◆ CAUTION
Compilers can and will reorder memory operations unless you use the appropriate memory barriers or atomics. volatile in C is not a substitute for atomic_thread_fence.
07 TYPOGRAPHY Headings · blockquotes · tables · lists · inline

Systems software is the layer where abstractions stop being free. Every added indirection has a price — paid in cycles, cache lines, and sometimes nights. The good news is that you can measure it. The better news is that most of the cost lives in a very small part of the code.

Memory hierarchy

Modern CPUs are fast. Memory is not. The entire game of systems performance is hiding that gap. An L1 cache hit costs ~4 cycles. A DRAM access costs ~200. If your hot path accesses memory linearly — sequentially, with spatial locality — you pay L1/L2 prices. If it chases pointers across the heap, you pay DRAM prices every time.

Slab allocators

The kernel uses slab allocation for frequently-allocated same-size objects. A slab is a contiguous chunk of pages carved into equal-sized slots. Free list tracking uses the slot itself as storage — no external bookkeeping.

The first rule of optimisation: don't. The second: don't yet. The third: profile before you touch anything.

Inline elements

You can use bold for emphasis, italic for names and titles, and monospace for code references inline. Links look like this anchor — accent-coloured, underlined. Inline math renders as $O(n \log n)$ without display break.

Tables

AllocatorAlloc costFree costFragmentation
BumpO(1), ~2 instrO(1) bulk onlyNone until reset
SlabO(1), pop freelistO(1), push freelistInternal only
BuddyO(log n)O(log n) + coalesceInternal + external
dlmallocO(1) avgO(1) avg + coalesceBoth, bounded

Lists

  • Linux mmap(MAP_ANONYMOUS) gives you zeroed pages from the OS
  • glibc's malloc uses brk() for small allocations, mmap for large
  • Zig's GeneralPurposeAllocator detects double-free and leaks in debug mode
  1. Profile with perf to find the hot path
  2. Check cache miss rate — if > 5%, data layout is the problem
  3. Restructure data for sequential access (AoS → SoA)
  4. Verify improvement — then stop

That's the full stack. Everything on this page renders correctly in both light and dark themes, with proper syntax highlighting, math rendering, and diagram support.
